strategy_summary: “A fly lands on a ceiling by flying up at an angle of about 45° with its front feet extended; as soon as contact is made the fly cartwheels over onto its other four feet.” (Foy and Oxford Scientific Films 1982:13)
mechanism: “A fly lands on a ceiling by flying up at an angle of about 45° with its front feet extended; as soon as contact is made the fly cartwheels over onto its other four feet.” (Foy and Oxford Scientific Films 1982:13)
